How powerful are right words! Saul was, for a time, convinced of the unreasonableness of his hatred toward David; but he continued his malice against David. So incurable is the hatred of the seed of the serpent against that of the woman; so deceitful and desperately wicked is the heart of man without the grace of God.

Michal's plan to gain time until David got to a distance was acceptable, but her lie had no excuse, and it shows that she was not influenced by the same spirit of piety which dictated Jonathan’s language to Saul. In going to Samuel, David made God his refuge. Samuel, as a prophet, was best able to advise him what to do in this day of distress. He found little rest or satisfaction in Saul's court, so he went to seek it in Samuel's church. What little pleasure is to be had in this world is for those who live a life of communion with God; to that David returned in the time of trouble. Saul was so impatient after David’s blood, so restless against him, that although thwarted by one providence after another, he could not see that David was under the special protection of God. And when God will take this way to protect David, even Saul prophesies. Many have great gifts, yet no grace; they may prophesy in Christ's name, yet are disowned by him. Let us daily seek for renewing grace, which shall be in us as a well of water springing up into everlasting life. Let us cleave to truth and holiness with full purpose of heart. In every danger and trouble, let us seek protection, comfort, and direction in God’s ordinances.
